Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results explicitly identify maize CER6 as UniProt B4G0N2.

Source_Species.reason: The paper refers to ZmCER6 as the maize CER6 partner of GL2.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Figure 3F defines Zm as Z. mays.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state genes were cloned from maize cDNA (B73).

Applied_Species.reason: The tested ZmCER6 protein is from maize.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Zm denotes Zea mays in the paper.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify maize cDNA (B73) as source for cloned genes used experimentally.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The study links CER6-GL2 activity to VLCFA elongation beyond C28 for cuticular wax biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: VLCFAs are fatty acids, explicitly discussed throughout.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Cuticular wax as a hydrophobic barrier is stated, but specific association with drought tolerance and pest resistance appears in broader discussion/implication language, not as a direct tested result for ZmCER6.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper frames VLCFAs as products for plant cuticular wax biosynthesis.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Results and title directly place ZmCER6 in cuticular wax biosynthesis / VLCFA elongation.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: ZmCER6-GL2 is shown to catalyze the condensation step of ER fatty acid elongation for >C28 chains.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Figure 1 and mechanistic model support condensation and >C28 chain extension when partnered with GL2.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports an important control role in chain-length specificity, but does not directly demonstrate or state rate-limiting behavior.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Figure 1 and text show direct formation of ketoacyl-CoA intermediates that lead to elongated VLCFA products.

Enzyme_Name.reason: ZmCER6 is identified as the maize ortholog of CER6/KCS6, i.e., 3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase 6.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state ZmCER6 has a FAE or β-ketoacyl-ACP synthase III domain and adopts a thiolase fold; it is a CER6/KCS-family enzyme.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Figure 1A and Results state the complex condenses acyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to generate elongated β-ketoacyl-CoA.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show ZmCER6-ZmGL2 preferentially elongates 30:0-CoA to 32:0-ketoacyl-CoA and that neither subunit alone showed activity.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are used in the paper on ZmCER6: insect-cell coexpression, LC-MS assays, cryo-EM, mutagenesis, and pull-downs.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point is directly supported by Results: catalytic partner role, GL2 dependence, structural features including Cys-His-Asn triad, and activity loss in catalytic mutants.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The complex is described as membrane-anchored in the ER fatty acid elongase system; ZmCER6 has transmembrane helices anchoring it in the membrane.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The experiments explicitly use in vitro assays with malonyl-CoA and 26:0/28:0/30:0-CoA substrates.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: ZmGL2 is shown structurally and biochemically to interact with ZmCER6.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Engineering wax composition and stress-resilient crops is proposed in title/abstract/discussion as implication, but not directly tested in Results.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state ZmCER6 was under AcMNPV polyhedrin promoter in pFastBac Dual for insect expression.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Both statements are supported: distinct Cys-His-Asn triad vs mammalian systems, and no detectable activity of ZmCER6 alone while AtCER6 retains intrinsic 26:0-CoA elongation activity.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results and Methods identify AtCER6 as UniProt Q9XF43.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly references Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species.reason: AtCER6 protein from Arabidopsis was tested in vitro as comparator.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AtCER6 is discussed as part of CER6-CER2 complexes involved in VLCFA elongation for cuticular wax biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Cuticular waxes are explicitly described as hydrophobic barriers.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The pathway is cuticular wax biosynthesis.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: AtCER6 is used as a comparative enzyme in cuticular wax / VLCFA elongation.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: AtCER6-AtCER2 is assayed for the condensation step in ER fatty acid elongation.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The in vitro assay shows condensation activity for VLCFA elongation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish AtCER6 as rate-limiting; 'key control step' is interpretive rather than directly shown.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: AtCER6-AtCER2 directly forms ketoacyl-CoA intermediates leading to VLCFAs.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CER6 is treated as a KCS/FAE-related enzyme; this family/domain assignment is consistent with the paper.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The assay data support condensation of VLC acyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to elongated β-ketoacyl-CoA.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Figure 1E-F assays 28:0-CoA and 26:0-CoA with malonyl-CoA for AtCER6/AtCER2.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state AtCER6-AtCER2 converts 28:0-CoA to 30:0-ketoacyl-CoA and AtCER6 alone shows intrinsic 26:0-CoA elongation activity.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: LC-MS-based in vitro enzyme assay of heterologously expressed proteins was performed.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These summary points accurately reflect the comparative Results for AtCER6.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: ER localization is biologically plausible and implied by discussion of the ER elongase system, but not directly shown experimentally for AtCER6 in this paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper explicitly used in vitro assays with malonyl-CoA and 26:0/28:0-CoA substrates.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: AtCER2 is coexpressed and assayed as the AtCER6 partner.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a proposed application not directly supported by a tested result for AtCER6.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods describe the same dual-promoter insect-cell expression system for AtCER6-AtCER2 as above, implying AtCER6 under AcMNPV polyhedrin promoter.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper uses AtCER6 primarily as an evolutionary/biochemical comparator to maize CER6.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results identify ZmGL2 as UniProt A0A804MAL4.

Source_Species.reason: ZmGL2 is the maize GL2 protein studied.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Zm denotes Z. mays in the paper.

Regulator_Type.reason: Results conclude GL2 acts as an auxiliary modulatory component and noncatalytic BAHD-family-like factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state ZmGL2 adopts the typical BAHD-enzyme fold with conserved HxxxD motif.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ZmGL2 is linked to VLCFA elongation beyond C28 for cuticular wax biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The terminal products discussed are VLCFAs, i.e., fatty acids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Cuticular wax hydrophobic barrier is explicit, but drought/pest resistance is only proposed application/implication, not directly demonstrated for ZmGL2.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The pathway context is cuticular wax biosynthesis.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results directly support control of chain-length extension and substrate specificity during VLCFA elongation.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The complex with ZmGL2 is required for elongation beyond C28 and detectable 30:0-CoA elongation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show ZmGL2 binding is required for detectable 30:0-CoA to 32:0-ketoacyl-CoA activity and structural accommodation of >C28 chains is proposed from structure/mutagenesis.

Regulation_Mode.reason: The paper explicitly describes GL2 as noncatalytic/allosteric-like modulator that remodels the substrate tunnel via complex formation.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Structural results indicate ZmGL2 acts on the ZmCER6 substrate tunnel/interface.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: ZmGL2 directly interacts with ZmCER6 by cryo-EM and pull-down assays.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper states the ZmCER6-ZmGL2 complex has 2:2 stoichiometry with a ZmCER6 homodimer and two ZmGL2 molecules.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: ZmGL2 binds on the cytosolic side of the complex structurally, but ER localization per se is implied from the complex context rather than directly shown by localization experiment.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state ZmGL2 was cloned under the AcMNPV p10 promoter.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are used directly for ZmGL2.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These points accurately summarize direct Results on ZmGL2 complex formation, channel remodeling, interface mutants, and HxxxD motif dispensability.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: In vitro assays with malonyl-CoA and VLC acyl-CoA substrates are directly reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Engineering value is a discussion implication, not a directly tested result.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly shows the HxxxD motif is spatially separated from the CER6 active site and mostly dispensable except G172A partially affects stability/activity.

Applied_Species.reason: AtCER2 protein was directly tested in the Arabidopsis comparator complex.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Comparative Results indicate CER2/GL2 homologs reprogram CER6 substrate specificity.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The paper concludes CER2/GL2 homologs act as evolutionary switches reprogramming CER6 substrate specificity; AtCER6-AtCER2 supports 28:0 to 30:0-ketoacyl-CoA conversion.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: AtCER2 is coexpressed and assayed with AtCER6 as a complex.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The AtCER6-AtCER2 elongase complex is directly assayed.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: LC-MS-based in vitro assay of heterologously expressed AtCER6-AtCER2 complex was performed.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These summary points reflect the direct comparative Results and interpretation given in the paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper assayed AtCER6-AtCER2 with malonyl-CoA and VLC acyl-CoA substrates.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a speculative application not directly tested for AtCER2.
